title: Group-relative numeric medians
summary: Subtract target-free group baselines from core numeric columns while avoiding future driver-race sequence statistics.
rationale: Context-normalized numeric deltas appeared in many strong preprocessing stacks, but full-race future summaries are risky under the task’s causal ordering. A safer version uses same-lap race context and broad compound/year baselines rather than future driver-specific information.
implementation_hint: For same-lap race groups, add current-row-minus-race-lap medians for pace, degradation, tyre age, and position. For broad non-sequential groups such as `Compound` or `Year`, add target-free global median deltas. Avoid within-driver-race future summaries.
expected_effect: Small to medium ROC AUC gain with medium-high confidence.
risk: Broad global medians are target-free but still transductive in a static table; avoid using future rows within a driver-race sequence.
